data.title: EU Considers Reopening Energy Measures Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The European Union is contemplating the reactivation of energy measures initially implemented following Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine. Bloomberg posted on X that this move comes as the bloc aims to address a new price shock linked to the ongoing conflict involving Iran. The EU's decision reflects its ongoing efforts to stabilize energy markets and ensure security amid geopolitical tensions. The measures, which were previously used to mitigate the impact of the Ukraine crisis, may be adapted to address the current situation. The EU's approach underscores the importance of coordinated action in response to global energy challenges.

data.title: BitGo Launches Portfolio-Based Financing Platform for Institutional Clients
data.description: BitGo has introduced a new portfolio-based financing platform designed for institutional clients to borrow and lend against digital assets from a single account. According to NS3.AI, the platform accommodates liquid tokens, staked positions, and locked holdings as collateral within BitGo’s custody environment. BitGo ensures that collateral is stored in segregated wallets, allowing funds to be utilized for trading, liquidity, and treasury purposes.

data.title: Nvidia Closes Above $165 Amid Potential Breakdown Concerns
data.description: Nvidia's stock closed above $165 on March 30, following a decline of over 9% since March 25. According to NS3.AI, this positions the stock on a head-and-shoulders neckline, suggesting an 11% potential breakdown if it fails to hold. The put-call volume ratio decreased to 0.74 by March 30 from 0.89 on March 25, indicating increased call activity during the downturn.

data.title: SWIFT to Enforce Structured Address Data in Payment Messages by November 2026
data.description: SWIFT will begin rejecting payment messages containing unstructured address data starting November 14, 2026. According to NS3.AI, this move is part of SWIFT's broader initiative to ensure the complete adoption of the ISO 20022 standard by financial institutions. Previously, SWIFT retired its legacy MT payment messages on November 22, 2025. The transition to structured data aims to minimize manual processing errors and enhance connectivity with blockchain-based settlement networks.

data.title: Fed's Schmid: Iran Conflict Likely to Intensify Inflation Pressure
data.description: According to Jin10, Federal Reserve official Schmid has expressed concerns that the ongoing conflict in Iran could exacerbate inflationary pressures. The situation in the region is being closely monitored as it may have significant implications for global economic stability. Schmid's remarks highlight the potential for increased volatility in markets as geopolitical tensions rise. The Federal Reserve is expected to consider these developments in its upcoming policy discussions.

data.title: Fed's Schmid: U.S. Economy Bolstered by Strong Demand and Low Unemployment
data.description: According to Jin10, Federal Reserve official Schmid highlighted several positive factors supporting the U.S. economy. These include robust demand momentum, improved productivity, and a relatively low unemployment rate. Schmid's remarks underscore the resilience of the U.S. economic landscape amid various global challenges.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Schmid: Policy Measures Essential to Strengthen Long-Term Inflation Expectations
data.description: Federal Reserve official Schmid emphasized the necessity for the Federal Reserve to implement policy measures to solidify robust long-term inflation expectations. According to Jin10, Schmid highlighted the importance of these actions in maintaining economic stability and ensuring that inflation expectations remain anchored. The statement underscores the Federal Reserve's commitment to addressing inflationary pressures and supporting sustainable economic growth.

data.title: Fed's Schmid: Inflation from Rising Oil Prices May Not Be Temporary
data.description: According to Jin10, Federal Reserve official Schmid has expressed concerns about the potential long-term impact of rising oil prices on inflation. Schmid emphasized that it is not safe to assume that the inflationary effects of increased oil prices will be temporary. This statement comes amid ongoing discussions about the global economic outlook and the factors influencing inflation rates. Schmid's remarks highlight the need for careful monitoring of energy markets and their influence on broader economic conditions.

data.title: Bitcoin Rises Amid Unconfirmed Reports of Iran Seeking Security Guarantees
data.description: Bitcoin's price increased to $67,762 following reports suggesting that Iran might be open to ending the conflict if it receives security guarantees. According to NS3.AI, these remarks remain unconfirmed. Meanwhile, WTI crude oil prices decreased to $102 from just under $105 as concerns over broader disruptions subsided.

data.title: Amber Group CEO Reflects on Impact of FTX Collapse
data.description: Amber Group CEO Michael Wu described the 2022 FTX collapse as the most significant setback of his career. According to NS3.AI, the incident led to Amber's potential $10 billion valuation plummeting. Wu noted that before the crisis, Amber employed 1,300 people globally, but the company subsequently faced severe downsizing and quarterly financial losses.

data.title: Iranian President Expresses Readiness to End Conflict with Conditions
data.description: Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi has announced the country's willingness to conclude ongoing hostilities, contingent upon receiving certain assurances. According to Odaily, Raisi emphasized the need for guarantees to ensure a peaceful resolution. The statement reflects Iran's strategic approach to international diplomacy, seeking stability while addressing security concerns.

data.title: MagicEden Wallet to Enter Export-Only Mode and Be Removed from App Stores
data.description: MagicEden's wallet is set to transition to an export-only mode and will be removed from app stores tomorrow, according to Odaily. Users who lose access to their devices or the downloaded app version will be unable to access any wallets.

data.title: Iranian President: Willing to end the war with guarantees
data.description: Iranian President Manmohan Pezechyan stated that Iran has no intention of starting a war, but is willing to end it if it can ensure that it will not be attacked again. Pezechyan called the military actions of the United States and Israel unprecedented crimes and violations of international law, and said that Iran had engaged in goodwill negotiations before the attacks. He also stated that countries hosting US military bases failed to prevent these bases from being used to attack Iran. Pezechyan pointed out that ceasing aggression is the way forward, and that Iran is willing to end the conflict if security guarantees are provided; he urged Europe to abandon its current practices and called for professional cooperation consistent with international law. (Jinshi)

data.title: Brent crude oil plunged 6.00% intraday, currently trading at $101.40 per barrel.
data.description: According to Gate data, Brent crude oil plunged 6.00% intraday, currently trading at $101.40 per barrel. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iranian President: We are prepared to end the war, but we want guarantees.
data.description: Iranian President Peskov: We are prepared to end the war, but we hope to receive guarantees. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. stocks rallied in the short term, with the Nasdaq rising over 3%, and the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both gaining over 2%.
data.description: US stocks rallied in the short term, with the Nasdaq rising over 3%, and the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both gaining over 2%. Earlier reports indicated that the Iranian president stated, "We are prepared to end the war, but we hope to receive guarantees."

data.title: Increase in Ship Traffic Through Hormuz Strait Amid Iranian Blockade
data.description: The number of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz is gradually increasing, despite Iran's ongoing blockade of most unauthorized commercial vessels. According to Odaily, Bloomberg's compiled tracking data shows that as of Monday, the average weekly two-way transit of ships rose to seven, up from five the previous week. Although this is still far from normal traffic levels, the increase indicates that a selective fleet, primarily from non-hostile Asian countries, continues to navigate the port. By allowing friendly ships to pass while restricting Western vessels, Iran's blockade has evolved into a potent economic warfare tool.

data.title: Benchmark Sets Buy Rating for Cantor Equity Partners II Amid Securitize Merger Plans
data.description: Benchmark has initiated coverage on Cantor Equity Partners II, assigning a Buy rating in light of its planned merger with Securitize. According to NS3.AI, Benchmark analysts have set a price target of $16 for Securitize, contingent upon the company achieving $178 million in sales by the end of next year. The analysts emphasized that this target also relies on Securitize expanding its competitive advantage through strategic partnerships with blue-chip companies.

data.title: During the blockade, the number of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz with Iranian approval increased.
data.description: The number of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz and the strength of their signals are slowly increasing, although Iran currently maintains an effective blockade against most unlicensed commercial vessels. According to tracking data compiled by Bloomberg, the weekly average number of ships passing through in both directions rose to seven as of Monday, up from five the previous week. While still far from normal traffic levels, this increase indicates that a small, selective fleet—primarily from non-hostile Asian countries—is continuing to transit the port. By allowing friendly vessels to pass while restricting Western vessels, Iran's blockade has evolved into a powerful tool of economic warfare. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iran and Turkey Discuss Regional Developments Amid Tensions
data.description:
Iranian Foreign Minister Aragchi and Turkish Foreign Minister Fidan held a phone conversation to discuss the latest regional developments. According to RTHK, Aragchi emphasized the responsibility of governments to condemn acts of aggression, particularly those targeting schools, research centers, historical and cultural sites, production facilities, energy infrastructure, and residential areas. Aragchi criticized U.S. officials for publicly threatening to attack Iran's energy and production facilities, labeling such statements as criminal threats that disregard international law and human rights principles. He called for a firm response from the international community.

During the conversation, Fidan referenced the recent quadrilateral foreign ministers' meeting in Islamabad, Pakistan, highlighting the regional countries' support for ending war and achieving peace and stability.
